product: GLM-L2-LCFA  (Geostationary Lightning Mapper, Level-2, Lightning Cluster-Filter Algorithm)

operational_buckets_verified_2026-06-13.noaa-goes16: EXISTS but NO GLM data under 2026/164 (DOY today) -- decommissioned/archive only, DO NOT use for live
operational_buckets_verified_2026-06-13.noaa-goes17: EXISTS but NO GLM data under 2026/164 (DOY today) -- archive only, DO NOT use for live

s3.DO_NOT_HARDCODE_G16: GOES-16 is no longer the operational East GLM source; East is G19 as of 2025+. Verify by listing GLM-L2-LCFA/{Y}/{DOY}/ for today before trusting.

timestamp_fields.s: scan/coverage START time, format YYYYDDDHHMMSSf (year, day-of-year, hour, min, sec, f=tenths-of-second). s2026164 1903400 => 2026 DOY164 19:03:40.0
timestamp_fields.e: coverage END time same format; END - START = ~20 s exactly per granule
timestamp_fields.c: file CREATION time same format (~within seconds after e)

s3.granule_cadence: ~20 s per granule => ~180 files/hour per satellite. New file LastModified lags wall-clock by ~20-40 s.
s3.latest_granule_logic: list_objects_v2 on prefix GLM-L2-LCFA/{Y}/{DDD}/{HH}/ ; keys sort lexicographically == chronological by the s-tag, so Contents[-1] is newest in that hour. For 'last N minutes' span hours if the window crosses an hour boundary (also fetch previous hour at top of hour). Parse the s-tag (chars after '_s') to get exact start time; filter granules whose [s,e] window overlaps the requested window.

decode.venv_status_2026-06-13: PROJECT VENV CANNOT READ THIS FILE AS-IS. Installed backends were only scipy + cfgrib; scipy handles netCDF3 only and FAILS ('not a valid NetCDF 3 file'). netCDF4, h5netcdf, h5py all MISSING.
decode.NEW_DEPENDENCY_REQUIRED: Add BOTH 'h5netcdf' AND 'h5py' (h5netcdf does NOT pull h5py automatically -> ImportError 'No module named h5py'). Alternatively a single 'netCDF4' dep also works but is a heavier wheel.
decode.verified_working_engine: xarray.open_dataset(path, engine='h5netcdf') with h5py present -- CONFIRMED decodes the real granule.
decode.cf_decoding: Leave default decode_cf=True: xarray auto-unpacks int16 scale_factor/add_offset to physical units and converts all *_time / *_offset vars to datetime64[ns].

point_radius_query_vars.flash_lat: coord, dim=(number_of_flashes,), float32, units=degrees_north (stored directly as float32, no scaling)
point_radius_query_vars.flash_lon: coord, dim=(number_of_flashes,), float32, units=degrees_east (-180..180, negative=W; sample range -128..-34)
point_radius_query_vars.flash_time_offset_of_first_event: coord, dim=(number_of_flashes,), decoded datetime64[ns]; RAW int16 'seconds since {time_coverage_start}'
point_radius_query_vars.flash_energy: data_var, dim=(number_of_flashes,), units=J; RAW int16 scale_factor=9.9999e-16 add_offset=2.8515e-16 _FillValue=-1
point_radius_query_vars.event_lat: coord, dim=(number_of_events,), units=degrees_north; RAW int16 scale_factor=0.00203128 add_offset=-66.56 (xarray unpacks)
point_radius_query_vars.event_lon: coord, dim=(number_of_events,), units=degrees_east; RAW int16 with scale_factor/add_offset
point_radius_query_vars.event_time_offset: coord, dim=(number_of_events,), datetime64[ns]; RAW int16 'seconds since {time_coverage_start}'
point_radius_query_vars.group_lat_lon: group_lat/group_lon coords (number_of_groups,) units degrees_north/east; intermediate aggregation level

time_coverage.product_time: scalar, J2000 epoch ('seconds since 2000-01-01 12:00:00'), = coverage start; decodes to 2026-06-13T19:03:40
time_coverage.GOTCHA_two_epochs: product_time uses J2000 epoch; flash/event/group *_time_offset use PER-FILE epoch 'seconds since {time_coverage_start}'. Do not assume one epoch. xarray decode_cf handles each var's own units string.

global_attrs.title: GLM L2 Lightning Detections: Events, Groups, and Flashes

sentinels_and_gotchas: _FillValue=-1 on flash_energy/event_energy/group_energy (raw int16). Mask with _FillValue, not the displayed valid_range.
valid_range on energy shows as [0,-6] due to uint16 stored-as-signed display artifact (-6 == 65530 unsigned); do NOT use it for masking.
lon is degrees_east in -180..180 with negatives for Western Hemisphere (NOT 0..360). GOES-East flashes are ~-130..-30.
GLM only reports lightning OPTICALLY visible to the geostationary sensor; full-disk Americas coverage. Off-Earth/space-look pixels excluded.
number_of_flashes can be small or even 0 in quiet granules (500 here, hard cap-ish); events >> flashes (16763 here). Handle empty-dim granules.
Files are ~180-630 KB (G19 sample 632615 B; G18 sample 180028 B). Cheap to fetch many.
Quality flags (flash_quality_flag/group_quality_flag) float32; 0 == good_quality_qf per GLM spec -- filter if strict.
S3 keys sort == chronological by s-tag, so Contents[-1] of an hour prefix is newest; but a 'last 5 min' query near top-of-hour must also list the previous HH prefix.
